idea-plugin.name: Pixel Office - AI Agent Visualizer
idea-plugin.description:
Pixel Agents for IntelliJ

Turn your Claude Code terminal sessions into animated pixel art characters in a virtual office. Each terminal you open spawns a character that walks around, sits at desks, and visually reflects what the agent is doing in real time.

Features

  • One agent, one character — every Claude Code terminal gets its own animated character
  • Live activity tracking — characters animate based on what the agent is doing (writing, reading, running commands)
  • Sub-agent visualization — Task/Agent sub-agents spawn as separate characters, including async background agents
  • Office layout editor — design your office with floors, walls, and 80+ furniture items
  • Multiple themes — default office, alien, cat, and zoo themes with unique characters and furniture
  • Speech bubbles — visual indicators when an agent is waiting or needs permission
  • Sound notifications — optional chime when an agent finishes its turn
  • Persistent layouts — office design saved across IDE restarts
  • Animated furniture — wall clocks tick, desk fans spin, water coolers bubble

How It Works

Pixel Agents watches Claude Code's JSONL transcript files to track what each agent is doing. No modifications to Claude Code are needed — it's purely observational. The webview runs a lightweight game loop with canvas rendering, BFS pathfinding, and a character state machine.

Getting Started

  1. Install Claude Code CLI
  2. Open the Pixel Agents tool window (bottom panel)
  3. Click + Agent to spawn a Claude Code terminal
  4. Watch the character react as Claude works on your code

Based on Pixel Agents for VS Code by Pablo De Lucca. Ported to the IntelliJ Platform with additional features.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0

  • Initial release — IntelliJ Platform port of Pixel Agents
  • JCEF-based webview with full canvas rendering
  • Claude Code JSONL transcript watching with hybrid polling
  • Async sub-agent file tracking (separate JSONL files)
  • Multiple themes: default, alien, cat, zoo
  • Layout editor with undo/redo, floor/wall painting, furniture placement
  • Auto-animated furniture (clocks, fans, water coolers)
  • Agent persistence across IDE restarts
  • External session adoption (terminals started without --session-id)
  • Dead session detection and cleanup
